opp.name: FreshDirect Sourcing + SRM + Intake Opp_May'26
opp.next_step: JRA 5/20: ANA and Merlin Intake videos sent over from Agentic AI Procurement Summit. Discovery call to follow.
opp.ais_why:
* AI is the reason this deal exists: per Salesforce, "FreshDirect is exploring AI driven procurement automation after Lokesh researched AI integration in procurement and came across Zycus" - an inbound, AI-motivated entry.
* The stated need explicitly calls for AI: "better automation, visibility, and AI enabled support for indirect procurement," and the engagement was seeded by "ANA and Merlin Intake videos... from Agentic AI Procurement Summit."
* It remains early-funnel: the engagement is at Qualified (10%) with a discovery call still to come and no POC or budget yet - inbound AI interest that hasn't been converted into an active evaluation.

items.quote: One introductory call 19 May 2026 completed but not recorded. No follow-up discovery call, no stakeholder conversations, no LastActivityDate.
No business problem, pain, metrics, decision criteria, decision process, paper process, or champion documented. Only Decision Maker role assigned (Emre Turkcan) with no engagement evidence.
Four competitors logged (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, Ariba) with no buyer sentiment, positioning, or threat-level intelligence.
No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c populated despite AI-heavy product scope (Merlin Intake, ANA).

sources: Avoma manifest: introductory call 19 May 2026, completed but not recorded — no content
metrics.narrative: No business problem, pain quantification, or ROI case documented. The single introductory call on 19 May 2026 (38 days ago) was not recorded, so no buyer-stated objectives or quantified impact are available. No Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) were populated. Without a recorded discovery conversation or follow-up engagement, we have no understanding of FreshDirect's procurement pain points, cost savings targets, cycle time improvements, or strategic drivers. The immediate move is to secure a discovery call with Emre Turkcan (Decision Maker) and Lokesh Patturu (Evaluator) to surface and quantify the business case.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Lokesh Patturu (Evaluator), Chakib Atmane (Evaluator), no Champion role assigned
champion.narrative: No champion identified or developed. The contact roster includes Lokesh Patturu (Indirect Procurement Analyst, Evaluator) and Chakib Atmane (Manager Indirect Procurement, Evaluator), but there is no evidence of advocacy, internal navigation, or active engagement from either. No calls, emails, or tasks logged with Patturu or Atmane post-creation. The X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c field is unpopulated, and no OpportunityContactRole is marked Champion. We do not know if either evaluator has access to Emre Turkcan (Decision Maker), if they are pushing Zycus internally, or if they are even still engaged 38 days post-introduction. The gap is total: no champion strength, no advocacy evidence, no internal intelligence. The immediate move is to reach out to Lokesh Patturu (the analyst-level evaluator most likely to engage day-to-day) and build a relationship — ask for his view on the evaluation timeline, the competitive shortlist, and who else we should be talking to. Then assess whether Patturu can champion us or if we need to cultivate Atmane (the manager-level evaluator).

sources: SF Competitors__c: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba (logged at creation)
SF Next_Step__c: videos sent 20 May, no follow-up
competition.narrative: Four competitors logged in Salesforce at deal creation: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba (SF Competitors__c). This is a crowded, four-way competitive field typical of enterprise sourcing deals in this segment. However, we have zero intelligence on their positioning, buyer sentiment, pricing, or threat level because no discovery or demo conversations have been recorded post-creation. We do not know if FreshDirect is running a formal RFP, if any competitor is the incumbent (SF Replacing_What__c and Existing_vendor__c are null), or which rival is winning on fit, price, or relationship. The Next Step from 20 May mentions sending ANA and Merlin Intake videos from a summit, suggesting Zycus is emphasizing the AI/agentic differentiation, but we have no evidence the videos were consumed or resonated. The immediate move is to run a discovery call to surface which competitors FreshDirect is actively evaluating, what their perceived strengths are, and where Zycus can wedge on Merlin Intake's AI negotiation and ANA's agentic orchestration against the commoditized ERP-anchored suites (Ariba, Coupa).

identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain articulated by the relevant stakeholder single introductory call on 19 May 2026 was not recorded, so no buyer-stated pain points are available. The Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, and What_if_this_is_not_done__c fields are unpopulated. We do not know if FreshDirect is replacing an incumbent (Salesforce Replacing_What__c is null), addressing supplier complexity, reducing maverick spend, or targeting cycle time improvements. The product scope (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin Intake, ANA) suggests broad sourcing and procurement automation, but the pain owner, urgency, and consequences of inaction are unknown. The immediate move is to run a discovery call with Emre Turkcan (Decision Maker) and Lokesh Patturu (Evaluator) to surface the specific procurement challenges, who owns the pain, and what happens if they do not solve it by the August close date.

sources: Avoma: no recorded contracting or procurement discussions
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics, legal review, InfoSec requirements, or procurement workflow documented. We do not know if FreshDirect requires MSA/SOW redlining, a formal security questionnaire, ERP integration scoping, or partner/SI involvement. The Mandate__c and Current_Contract_Expiration__c fields are unpopulated, so there is no known forcing function or incumbent contract deadline. Given the product scope (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA), implementation and integration will be significant, but no scoping has occurred. The gap is total: no visibility into legal, InfoSec, or procurement channel. The immediate move is to surface the paper process in the discovery call — ask about procurement approval steps, legal review cycle, InfoSec requirements, and any incumbent contract expiration that creates urgency.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Emre Turkcan, Sr. Director Indirect Procurement, role Decision Maker
Avoma: no recorded conversation with Turkcan
economic_buyer.narrative: Emre Turkcan, Sr. Director Indirect Procurement, is listed as Decision Maker in the contact roles (SF OpportunityContactRole). However, there is no evidence of engagement with Turkcan post-creation — no calls, no emails, no tasks logged. The Who_will_approve_budget__c and Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c fields are unpopulated, so the full approval chain and commercial authority are unknown. For a $200k deal, Turkcan's title suggests budget authority, but we have not confirmed active sponsorship, pricing conversations, or C-level sign-off requirements. The gap is that Turkcan has not been reached since the 19 May introduction (if he was even on that call — the content is not recorded), and we do not know if CFO or VP Supply Chain approval is required. The immediate move is to book a commercial scoping call with Turkcan to map the approval process and confirm his authority.

sources: Avoma: no call sequence or stakeholder progression visible
SF OpportunityContactRole: 3 contacts, no process context
decision_process.narrative: No decision process or approval workflow documented. The contact roster shows two Evaluators (Lokesh Patturu, Chakib Atmane) and one Decision Maker (Emre Turkcan), but we do not know if there is a formal vendor selection committee, a structured RFP round, or executive sign-off gates. The What_is_the_decision_process__c and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c fields are unpopulated. We have not observed who joins calls when, who escalates, or who drops off — the single introductory call on 19 May had no transcript, and no subsequent touchpoints exist. The gap is that we are flying blind on how FreshDirect will shortlist, select, and approve a vendor. The immediate move is to ask Turkcan or Patturu to walk us through their sourcing and approval process, including timelines for RFP, demos, vendor selection, and contracting, given the four-way competitive field.

sources: Avoma: no recorded discovery or demo content
decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria, RFP requirements, or decision framework documented. The single 19 May call (content not recorded) did not produce documented use-case needs, technical fit requirements, or selection scorecard. No Salesforce fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c) were populated. We do not know if FreshDirect is running a formal RFI/RFP process, what weight they place on AI (despite Merlin Intake and ANA being in scope), or what fitment criteria distinguish the four logged competitors. The gap is total: no ShoeFit/BRD alignment, no documented must-haves, no test-case scenarios. The immediate move is to secure a discovery call to surface their procurement challenges, technical requirements, and decision scorecard, then position Merlin Intake's AI negotiation and ANA's agentic orchestration as differentiators against the shortlist.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c populated, so no formal AI fit signal from Salesforce. However, the product scope (Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA) and the Next Step from 20 May (videos sent from Agentic AI Procurement Summit) suggest FreshDirect expressed at least curiosity about AI-driven procurement during the initial conversation. Without a recorded discovery call or AIS assessment, we cannot determine if FreshDirect is AI Hungry (actively seeking agentic capabilities), AI Curious (open but unvalidated), or AI Resistant (skeptical of AI ROI). The immediate move is to assess AI appetite in the discovery call and complete an AIS evaluation.

Next Step from 20 May shows Justin Ajmo sent ANA and Merlin Intake videos and noted a discovery call to follow, but no evidence the discovery call occurred. Last modified 18 Jun 2026 (administrative edit, not buyer engagement). No LastActivityDate. Zero movement for 38 days post-creation. The buyer has not responded to the video send or scheduled the follow-up discovery call. The deal is cold.

items.detail: 61 days to close (26 Aug 2026) with zero movement in 38 days post-creation. No discovery, no demos, no RFP, no vendor selection, no commercials, no contracting. At current pace, the deal cannot close by the relevant stakeholder date is a placeholder, not a buyer-driven deadline.

Four-way competitive field (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, Ariba) with zero intelligence on buyer sentiment, positioning, or threat levels. No discovery or demo conversations to differentiate Zycus or wedge on AI capabilities. High risk of competitive displacement if we remain dark while rivals engage.

No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. Emre Turkcan (Decision Maker) has not been contacted post-creation. Budget authority, approval chain, and C-level sponsorship unconfirmed. High risk the deal stalls at vendor selection or commercials without executive alignment.

confidence_signals.summary: No confidence signals. The deal is cold: zero buyer engagement for 38 days post-creation, no follow-up to the 20 May video send, no discovery call scheduled or delivered, no stakeholder conversations, no LastActivityDate. The single introductory call on 19 May was not recorded, so we have no content to assess sentiment or interest. The deal is at high risk of ghosting or competitive loss.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 19 May 2026 (38 days ago), close date 26 Aug 2026 (61 days out). Time in Qualified stage: 38 days with zero buyer engagement post-creation. No field history of stage progression, amount changes, or close date slips. No LastActivityDate on record. One introductory call 19 May (38 days ago, content not recorded), then complete silence. At current pace (zero movement in 38 days), the deal will not advance through discovery, demos, RFI/RFP rounds, shortlisting, vendor selection, commercials, negotiation, and contracting in 61 days. The buyer has not responded to the 20 May video send per Next Step. Forecast category Pipeline is appropriate given the dead air, but the close date is indefensible without immediate action.

north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified-stage deal remains completely dark 38 days post-creation with zero buyer engagement beyond the single 19 May intro call. No discovery, no stakeholder threading, no response to outreach—buyer has ghosted. Four competitors active by default.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal is dead in the water 38 days post-creation with zero buyer engagement, no discovery, and a four-way competitive field. The single highest-leverage action is for Justin Ajmo to re-engage FreshDirect immediately — reach out to Lokesh Patturu (the analyst-level evaluator most likely to respond) and Emre Turkcan (Decision Maker) to restart the conversation, surface their evaluation timeline, and position Zycus's AI differentiation. Without multi-threading and champion development, this deal will remain single-point-of-failure and ghost-prone. The AI story (Merlin Intake, ANA) is the wedge against the commoditized competitors, but it must be delivered live in discovery and demo, not via videos sent into the void.

competitive_position.summary: FreshDirect logged a four-way competitive shortlist at deal creation on 19 May 2026: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba. With zero recorded discovery or demo conversations since then, we have no intelligence on positioning, buyer sentiment, or threat levels. All four are active by default given the lack of elimination evidence. Coupa is listed first in the Competitors__c field and is the most recognized brand in this segment, so it is the presumed front-runner until we learn otherwise. GEP, JAGGAER, and Ariba are credible enterprise sourcing platforms with strong installed bases and ERP integration stories. Zycus's wedge is the AI/agentic differentiation (Merlin Intake's negotiation agent, ANA's orchestration) against the incumbents' commoditized workflow automation, but this story has not been delivered live — only via videos sent 20 May with no confirmed consumption. The immediate move is to run discovery and surface which competitors FreshDirect is actively demoing, what their perceived strengths are, and where we can position Merlin's AI capabilities as the next-gen leap.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Logged in Competitors__c at deal creation 19 May 2026; no buyer intelligence since.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin Intake's AI-driven negotiation and ANA's agentic orchestration as the next-gen procurement intelligence layer Coupa's workflow automation lacks. Make AI the centerpiece of the discovery and demo.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Logged in Competitors__c at deal creation 19 May 2026; no buyer intelligence since.
competitors.how_we_win: GEP strong on consulting-led transformation but weaker on embedded AI. Differentiate on Merlin's autonomous negotiation vs GEP's manual analytics.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: Logged in Competitors__c at deal creation 19 May 2026; no buyer intelligence since.
competitors.how_we_win: JAGGAER solid on sourcing workflow but lacks AI depth. Position Merlin Intake and ANA as the intelligent layer JAGGAER cannot match.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Logged in Competitors__c at deal creation 19 May 2026; no buyer intelligence since.
competitors.how_we_win: Ariba is the ERP-anchored incumbent play. Wedge on agility, AI innovation (Merlin/ANA), and user experience vs Ariba's heavy, complex deployment.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, and ANA, signaling AI/agentic differentiation is central to the deal. Justin Ajmo sent ANA and Merlin Intake videos from a summit on 20 May 2026 (per Next Step), suggesting Zycus is leading with the AI story. However, with no recorded discovery or demo conversations, we have no evidence the AI narrative landed, resonated, or was validated against FreshDirect's requirements. The AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are unpopulated, so we cannot assess buyer AI appetite. The risk is that the AI story was sent into the void (videos with no follow-up) and has not been delivered live to stakeholders. The immediate move is to run a discovery call to surface FreshDirect's AI readiness, position Merlin Intake's negotiation agent and ANA's orchestration as the wedge against Coupa/GEP/JAGGAER/Ariba's commoditized workflow automation, and make AI the centerpiece of the demo.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Product scope is ambitious and AI-heavy: iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, Merlin Intake, and ANA. This signals FreshDirect is open to a full-suite procurement transformation with AI/agentic capabilities at the core. However, with zero recorded discovery or demo conversations, we have not validated fit against their actual business requirements, technical environment, or decision criteria. The Next Step from 20 May shows Justin Ajmo sent ANA and Merlin Intake videos from a summit, suggesting Zycus is leading with the AI story, but we have no evidence the videos were consumed, resonated, or aligned with FreshDirect's priorities. The immediate move is to run a discovery call to surface their procurement challenges, technical stack, integration requirements, and AI appetite, then validate that the scoped products (especially Merlin Intake and ANA) map to their must-haves.

* The stated need explicitly calls for AI: "better automation, visibility, and AI enabled support for indirect procurement," and the engagement was seeded by "ANA and Merlin Intake videos... from Agentic AI Procurement Summit."
* It remains early-funnel: the engagement is at Qualified (10%) with a discovery call still to come and no POC or budget yet - inbound AI interest that hasn't been converted into an active evaluation.

value.name: Chakib Atmane

value.title: Manager Indirect Procurement

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Logged in Competitors__c at deal creation 19 May 2026; no buyer intelligence since.
value.how_we_win: Position Merlin Intake's AI-driven negotiation and ANA's agentic orchestration as the next-gen procurement intelligence layer Coupa's workflow automation lacks. Make AI the centerpiece of the discovery and demo.

value.name: GEP Worldwide
value.quote: Logged in Competitors__c at deal creation 19 May 2026; no buyer intelligence since.
value.how_we_win: GEP strong on consulting-led transformation but weaker on embedded AI. Differentiate on Merlin's autonomous negotiation vs GEP's manual analytics.

value.name: JAGGAER
value.quote: Logged in Competitors__c at deal creation 19 May 2026; no buyer intelligence since.
value.how_we_win: JAGGAER solid on sourcing workflow but lacks AI depth. Position Merlin Intake and ANA as the intelligent layer JAGGAER cannot match.

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: Logged in Competitors__c at deal creation 19 May 2026; no buyer intelligence since.
value.how_we_win: Ariba is the ERP-anchored incumbent play. Wedge on agility, AI innovation (Merlin/ANA), and user experience vs Ariba's heavy, complex deployment.

value.detail: 61 days to close (26 Aug 2026) with zero movement in 38 days post-creation. No discovery, no demos, no RFP, no vendor selection, no commercials, no contracting. At current pace, the deal cannot close by the relevant stakeholder date is a placeholder, not a buyer-driven deadline.

value.flag: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY: 38 days of complete silence post-creation with 61 days to close. Justin Ajmo must reach out to Lokesh Patturu (Evaluator, analyst level, most likely to respond day-to-day) and Emre Turkcan (Decision Maker) this week to restart the conversation, confirm their evaluation timeline, and book a discovery call to surface business requirements and competitive positioning. Without engagement in the next 7 days, recommend disqualifying the deal or pushing close date to Q1 2027.

packets.subject: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY: 38 days of complete silence post-creation with 61 days to close. value.flag: MULTI-THREAD AND BUILD A CHAMPION: Currently single-threaded with no champion evidence. After re-engagement, immediately multi-thread to Chakib Atmane (Manager Indirect Procurement, Evaluator) and assess whether Lokesh Patturu or Chakib Atmane can advocate internally. Ask for introductions to other stakeholders (VP Supply Chain, IT/ERP integration lead, InfoSec). A champion is critical to navigate the four-way competitive field and push Zycus through RFP/vendor selection.

value.flag: DELIVER THE AI DIFFERENTIATION LIVE: Product scope includes Merlin Intake and ANA (AI negotiation and agentic orchestration), but the story was sent via videos on 20 May with no confirmed consumption or resonance. In the discovery call, lead with FreshDirect's procurement pain, then position Merlin Intake's autonomous negotiation and ANA's orchestration as the next-gen intelligence layer Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, and Ariba cannot match. Make AI the centerpiece of the demo and use-case validation to differentiate in the crowded field.
